brief_rows.thesis: GE Vernova appears to sit in a constrained grid equipment and transformer buildout layer, with filing-backed component specificity, pressure signals, and capacity-expansion evidence that may still be underappreciated.
brief_rows.value_capture_layer: Constrained upstream component supplier with system-level leverage

brief_rows.research_priority_note: Confirm whether constrained component evidence is converting into pricing power, backlog durability, and capacity leverage.
research_questions: How much of current demand is tied specifically to transformers, switchgear, or substation equipment?
Is backlog or RPO converting into revenue without margin erosion?
Are new capacity additions likely to relieve or reinforce the current constrained layer?
top_catalysts: order backlog conversion into reported revenue
top_invalidations: capacity expansion arrives without meaningful demand conversion
component or backlog signals fade in subsequent company reporting
downstream demand shifts to substitute suppliers or adjacent architectures
why_missed: market attention is concentrated on downstream power and AI beneficiaries
constrained component-layer suppliers are less obvious from headline buildout coverage

brief_rows.thesis: Eaton appears to be a relevant beneficiary of grid equipment and transformer buildout, but the current evidence supports a beneficiary posture more cleanly than a strict bottleneck claim.
brief_rows.value_capture_layer: Relevant supply-chain beneficiary without strict bottleneck proof

brief_rows.research_priority_note: Confirm whether supplier exposure is large enough to matter economically even without strict chokepoint status.
research_questions: Which business segments are most exposed to the theme and how material are they?
Is capacity expansion targeted at the constrained layer or spread across unrelated end markets?
What would need to happen for this name to graduate from beneficiary to bottleneck candidate?
top_catalysts: order backlog conversion into reported revenue
top_invalidations: capacity expansion arrives without meaningful demand conversion
component or backlog signals fade in subsequent company reporting
downstream demand shifts to substitute suppliers or adjacent architectures
why_missed: beneficiary role is real but not yet framed as a strict chokepoint thesis
supplier relevance is easier to miss when coverage emphasizes end-market demand instead of component exposure
